title: Why Is Autism More Common in Males?
blurb: Harvard researcher Maya Talukdar argues that X chromosome genes reduce female risk for autism and potentially protect against other developmental disorders.

title: Primary Care “Opioid Risk Scores” Poorly Predict Individual OD Risk
blurb: External evaluation finds that an opioid risk score used in primary care poorly predicts individual overdose or opioid use disorder risk.

title: Partnering With Methadone Clinics “Transforms” Hep C Care, Study Finds
blurb: A randomized controlled trial concluded that offering hepatitis C treatment within opioid treatment programs greatly improved access and outcomes.

title: Fighting Breast Cancer in Rural America
blurb: Jen Cruz's PhD research examines how more precise approaches could improve breast cancer screening and public health interventions in rural communities.

title: Campus Harm Reduction Fueled by Student Advocacy, Some State Policies
blurb: Together, student advocacy and state policies have expanded harm reduction resources on college campuses.

title: Discontinued Canadian Safe Supply Program Was Working, Study Shows
blurb: A discontinued Canadian safer supply program was producing positive outcomes, according to a new study.

title: Aviation prodigy Cromwell Dixon's career took flight over Columbus
blurb: The first person to fly across the Continental Divide built his airships right by Ohio State's campus—and he was just a teenager.

title: Naloxone Boxes: New Strategy Makes OD Antidote Accessible 24/7, for Free
blurb: Inexpensive naloxone box programs make overdose reversal medication freely available in public spaces around the clock.

title: To Move Beyond Abstinence-Only, We Need Better Tools to Measure Recovery
blurb: It's time to develop more holistic, person-centered tools to measure recovery from substance use disorder.

title: Drug Testing Defeats the Purpose of “Second Chance” Hiring
blurb: Workplace drug testing can undermine second-chance hiring efforts for people with prior convictions.

title: Indigenous OD Mortality Plummets in Minnesota, But Disparity Remains
blurb: There's been a decline in Indigenous overdose mortality in Minnesota, but major disparities persist.

title: Tennessee Harm Reductionist Faces Charges for Drug Checking
blurb: A Tennessee harm reductionist was arrested for drug checking, showing the legal risks facing people involved in drug checking work.

title: People Seeking Methadone or Bupe Face “More Stress Than You Know”
blurb: A new study reports on the barriers people face when trying to access substance use disorder treatment.

title: Harm Reduction Vending Machines Work, Says 30 Years of Research
blurb: A new meta-analysis synthesizes decades of research on harm reduction vending machines—and finds encouraging results.

title: Music Festival Attendees Embrace DIY Drug Checking Despite Barriers
blurb: At Colorado's Sonic Bloom festival, attendees use do-it-yourself drug checking methods despite practical and legal barriers.

title: Test Strips Do Tell You If Xylazine Is Present, But Prone to False Positives
blurb: New research finds that xylazine test strips reliably detect xylazine but may also produce false positives.

title: Secret Shoppers Struggle to Buy Naloxone, Syringes at Texas Pharmacies
blurb: A harrowing new study sent secret shoppers to test access to over-the-counter naloxone and non-prescription syringes in Texas pharmacies.

title: Community “Bad Batch” Alerts Contrast With Algorithmic OD Prevention
blurb: Grassroots alert systems and algorithmic overdose prevention approaches must learn to coexist.

title: What’s in the Drug “Strips” Common in Prisons and Jails?
blurb: Chemical analysis offers clues about the drug-soaked paper strips—and their risks.

title: In Rural Areas, People Who Use Drugs Face Particularly Severe Housing Crisis
blurb: Research finds exceptionally high rates of homelessness among rural people who use unregulated opioids or inject drugs.

title: BC Drug-Decrim Study Explores Problems With 2.5-Gram Threshold
blurb: British Columbia’s drug decriminalization policy has an inadequate possession threshold, research finds.

title: Safer Drug Supply on the Dark Web? It’s Complicated, Researchers Find
blurb: Buying drugs on the dark web may reduce some risks, but it depends—on a lot.

title: Obituaries Experiment Highlights Stigma Against Overdose Victims
blurb: Stigma affects the way overdose deaths are described and remembered in obituaries.
